meaning: adj.腐败的,败血病的,脓毒性的n.腐烂物
examples: A dirty cut may go septic.
A flake of plaster from the ceiling fell into his eye, which became septic.
In this case, because it is caused by bacterial toxins, it is called septic shock.
Big ITT mixing fans keep the bacteria, waste and oxygen churned up to keep the soup from going septic.
To turn this into a viable business, T2 decided to focus on the problem of septic shock.
